content: I've been thinking about getting a pet for my new place in Boston. I work from home mostly, studying coral reefs. It gets a bit lonely sometimes.
That sounds like a great idea! What kind of pet are you considering? Something low-maintenance would probably work well with your remote work schedule.
I was thinking about a cat. I used to have one as a kid in California before I moved to the Bay Area for work. I miss having an animal around.
Cats are wonderful companions! Since you're in Boston now, have you looked into any local shelters or adoption centers?
Not yet, but I've been researching. I'm also trying to balance it with my other hobbies - I do Brazilian jiu-jitsu three times a week and pottery classes on weekends.
That's quite a busy schedule! A cat would definitely be a good fit then. They're independent but still provide great companionship when you're home.
Exactly. Plus I've been getting into birdwatching more seriously lately. I set up a bird feeder outside my window and it's been amazing watching the local species.
That's a perfect combination! A cat by the window watching birds with you sounds delightful. Have you decided on any specific breeds or ages you're interested in?
I'm leaning toward adopting an adult cat, maybe 2-3 years old. I think they're often overlooked at shelters and I'd prefer one that's already settled in its personality.
That's very thoughtful of you! Adult cats are wonderful - they're usually calmer and their personalities are already developed. Do you have a name picked out yet?
I was thinking of naming it after a coral species since that's my field. Maybe Acropora or Porites. What do you think?
Those are unique and meaningful names! Porites could be a great choice - it's distinctive and has a nice ring to it. Plus it would be a conversation starter about your work.
